mechanism_narrative: Low and stable FEDFUNDS indicates accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital and supporting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.2% as of t-1mo is historically low
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains low but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat over a 1-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.9% as of t-1mo is elevated
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate healthy consumer demand and investment, signaling broad economic strength that boosts corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST show sustained growth as specified, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low GDP growth, any further deceleration in economic activity, as measured by GDPC1, exacerbates investor pessimism, leading to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13254.7 as of t-4mo, indicating moderate growth
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0% but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of such occurrences over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent industrial production growth reflects robust manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons of 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.2666 as of t-1mo, indicating ongoing production
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows a sustained upward trend for 6 consecutive months, but the S&P 500 5-day returns are negative on average during this period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a critical threshold signals overheating and potential central bank tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.729 as of t-2mo, indicating moderate inflation
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3.0% but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll employment growth and a declining unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.1% AND UNRATE 3-month average change < -0.1%
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows sustained growth and UNRATE declines as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 30% of triggered instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of housing market stress, a significant drop in housing starts (HOUST) signals a broader contraction in consumer confidence and investment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 626.0 as of t-2mo, indicating moderate housing activity
failure_condition: If HOUST drops by more than 10% month-over-month, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year period.
